level_signals: 5+ years of experience as a Product Manager
Leading cross-team initiatives from ideation to execution
Responsible for high-impact AI agent product with 100k+ conversations per week

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience as a Product Manager
Deep user empathy and a high bar for user experience
Experience leading cross-team initiatives from ideation to execution
Technical expertise: You've led technical products and/or have a background in computer science (or similar)
Strong written and verbal communication skills

scope.surface: User-facing AI conversational agent (100k+ conversations per week) within Support Experience group
scope.business_model: B2B and B2C financial infrastructure platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Conversational AI agent effectiveness vs. human agents
Volume and quality of AI-resolved support conversations
Speed and quality of continuous improvement loops from problems to AI solutions
core_responsibilities: Make Stripe's conversational AI agent as capable and effective as our best human agents by identifying customer problems and building solutions to them.
Be opinionated on the UX for conversational AI - the best experiences are a hybrid of UI and conversation - you'll need to identify the primitives we need to build to be successful.
Build, ship and iterate on AI skills, and help build the systems that enable the rest of Stripe to do the same.
Create continuous improvement loops from problems to scaled AI solutions and relentlessly accelerate those loops.
Redefine how we think about quality for agentic conversations
Partner with the Conversation UX and Platform teams to unlock novel capabilities for the AI agent.

green_flags: Clear and specific product surface with measurable scale (100k+ conversations per week)
Hands-on PM role with direct involvement in AI agent development (prompts, evals, skills, tools)
Encourages applicants who meet minimum requirements to apply even without preferred qualifications
Applied AI at the forefront of a high-impact domain (support automation at scale)
Partnership with dedicated UX and Platform teams signals organizational investment
red_flags: Location, remote eligibility, work authorization, and visa sponsorship are entirely absent from the JD
No preferred qualifications listed — unclear what differentiates a strong applicant beyond minimums
Ambiguity around whether the PM is expected to write code or just work hands-on with prompts/evals

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required and scope of responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from Stripe's public positioning as a financial infrastructure platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type (not stated in JD body; marked as unclear)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred as false given IC PM framing)
ambiguities: Technical expertise requirement states 'led technical products and/or have a background in computer science (or similar)' — the 'and/or' means either work experience or academic credential is sufficient; classified as hard given unconditional minimum requirement framing.
No location information provided in JD — could be onsite, hybrid, or remote; marked as unclear.
No preferred qualifications section despite JD stating 'preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement' — nothing was listed under that heading.
